query: Roman pasta all'Amatriciana with guanciale, tomato, pecorino romano, and chili

draft.title: Pasta all'Amatriciana
draft.description: Classic Roman pasta with guanciale, tomato, pecorino romano, and chili, perfect for a flavorful and easy Italian dinner.

steps.text: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil for the pasta.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the guanciale strips and cook until crispy and golden, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the red chili pepper to the skillet and cook for 1 minute to release flavor.
Pour in the white wine and let it reduce by half, about 2-3 minutes.
Add the canned peeled tomatoes, crush them with a spoon, and simmer the sauce gently for 10-12 minutes until slightly thickened. Season with salt and black pepper.
Cook the spaghetti in the boiling water until al dente according to package instructions, usually 8-10 minutes.
Reserve a cup of pasta water, then drain the spaghetti.
Add the drained spaghetti to the sauce and toss well over low heat. Add reserved pasta water as needed to loosen the sauce.
Remove from heat and stir in most of the grated pecorino romano, reserving some for serving.
Serve immediately, topped with remaining pecorino and freshly ground black pepper.

warnings: Guanciale may be difficult to find; pancetta can be used as a substitute but will alter flavor.
Adjust chili pepper quantity to desired spiciness.
